title: EPIC-1: Enable verified users to reach checkout
body:
## Summary

Ship end-to-end user auth with email verification so only verified users can place orders.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] User can sign up, verify email, log in, and access the dashboard.
- [ ] Unverified users cannot place orders.

## Notes

- Use the product requirements in `docs/GITHUB_ISSUES_MVP.md` as the source of truth.

title: EPIC-2: Build reliable contacts and validated addresses
body:
## Summary

Provide CRUD for contacts and addresses plus CSV import for MVP usability.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Users can manage contacts and addresses reliably.
- [ ] Address validation status is tracked before order usage.

## Notes

- Use the product requirements in `docs/GITHUB_ISSUES_MVP.md` as the source of truth.

title: EPIC-3: Deliver real template previews and a print-ready pipeline
body:
## Summary

Seed templates, let users edit messages and photos, render print-ready previews, and keep output immutable for orders.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A template can be previewed before selection.
- [ ] Print-ready rendering output is testable and stable.

## Notes

- Use the product requirements in `docs/GITHUB_ISSUES_MVP.md` as the source of truth.

title: EPIC-4: Turn moments rules into scheduled drafts with approvals
body:
## Summary

Create moments rules, compute next occurrences, generate drafts at lead time, and manage draft approval.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Creating a moment reliably generates a draft on schedule.
- [ ] Users can review, approve, skip, or snooze drafts.

## Notes

- Use the product requirements in `docs/GITHUB_ISSUES_MVP.md` as the source of truth.

title: EPIC-5: Email notifications for draft, payment, and shipping states
body:
## Summary

Notify users on key state transitions with deep links to relevant pages.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Emails are sent for draft-ready, payment-required, shipped, and failed states.
- [ ] Notifications include links back to the relevant workflow.

## Notes

- Use the product requirements in `docs/GITHUB_ISSUES_MVP.md` as the source of truth.

title: EPIC-6: Convert approved drafts into paid orders
body:
## Summary

Implement pricing and payment processing so approved drafts become paid orders.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Successful payment creates an order with correct totals.
- [ ] Orders cannot proceed without successful payment.

## Notes

- Use the product requirements in `docs/GITHUB_ISSUES_MVP.md` as the source of truth.

title: EPIC-7: Fulfill paid orders and track delivery status
body:
## Summary

Integrate provider order creation, upload assets, and synchronize shipment status.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Paid orders are submitted to the provider.
- [ ] Status updates are visible in the product and traceable internally.

## Notes

- Use the product requirements in `docs/GITHUB_ISSUES_MVP.md` as the source of truth.

title: EPIC-8: Give support tooling to resolve order issues
body:
## Summary

Provide internal support tooling for order inspection, resend, and template management.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Support can inspect orders without database access.
- [ ] Basic template management exists for admin workflows.

## Notes

- Use the product requirements in `docs/GITHUB_ISSUES_MVP.md` as the source of truth.

title: EPIC-9: GDPR basics and content safety for order generation
body:
## Summary

Provide GDPR export and deletion flows plus MVP content filtering for abusive or unsafe message content.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Users can request export and deletion of personal data.
- [ ] Blocked content cannot proceed into the order pipeline.

## Notes

- Use the product requirements in `docs/GITHUB_ISSUES_MVP.md` as the source of truth.

title: TASK: Select print, storage, and delivery assumptions for MVP
body:
## Objective

Choose the MVP print-provider assumptions, object-storage shape, and delivery lead-time model so downstream rendering and fulfillment work can proceed without rework.

## Checklist

- [ ] Evaluate print provider API quality and required asset formats.
- [ ] Confirm supported destinations and lead-time assumptions.
- [ ] Confirm S3-compatible artifact storage requirements.
- [ ] Record decisions as ADRs where architecture impact exists.

## Validation

- [ ] Decisions are written down in the repository and linked from the issue.

title: TASK: Finalize staging and production hosting configuration
body:
## Objective

Confirm the deployment targets and secret-management strategy for web, API, and worker services.

## Checklist

- [ ] Confirm Vercel, Railway, Postgres, Redis, and object storage choices.
- [ ] Create provider projects and environments.
- [ ] Define staging and production secret ownership.
- [ ] Link the final setup back to `docs/DEPLOYMENT_PLAN.md`.

## Validation

- [ ] Hosting targets and required secrets are explicitly documented.

title: TASK: Establish database and migration foundation for backend domains
body:
## Objective

Create the initial database and migration foundation so auth, contacts, templates, and orders can evolve on a stable persistence layer.

## Checklist

- [ ] Choose ORM or migration tool.
- [ ] Add migration workflow and environment wiring.
- [ ] Define the first schema boundaries for users and foundational entities.
- [ ] Document how local and deployed databases are managed.

## Validation

- [ ] Migrations run deterministically in local development and CI.

title: TASK: Scaffold auth module and email verification contract
body:
## Objective

Create the first auth and verification contract that will support gated checkout later.

## Checklist

- [ ] Define auth module boundaries.
- [ ] Define signup, login, verification, and reset flows.
- [ ] Establish token or session strategy.
- [ ] Define the frontend-to-API contract for auth flows.

## Validation

- [ ] The auth boundary is documented and represented in code structure.
